threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the Overground is a reliable option for getting anywhere on time. Bless your heart, you’ll learn soon enough.
Caught the N35 last weekend; a guy with a saxophone serenading everyone, but it was the owl outside that stole the show. Perfect harmony with the night.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I saw a wild rabbit munching on leftover döner in the park while a kid practiced breakdancing nearby. Classic Neukölln chaos.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you find a bar that serves cocktails in real fruit skins—think coconut shells for piña coladas. It’s a wild flavor and aesthetic twist.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local when they still call it 'Civic Center' and don’t flinch at seeing a dog lounging on the Muni tracks like it owns the place.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the Muni today, spotted a guy in full business attire, backpack, and slippers. Only in the Tenderloin can you mix hustle with laid-back vibes.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think every corner in Gràcia has a bar with cheap cervezas, but it's the hidden plazas and local bodegas that keep the true vibe alive.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them by their fascination with the street performers in Plaça del Sol—they’re the ones taking endless photos instead of just enjoying the show.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it the 'overground' instead of just 'the train'—everyone else knows it’s all the same once you’re off the Tube.
Caught a night bus from Brixton and overheard two lads discussing how they swear the old lady on the 35 talks to the ghosts that haunt the route.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I heard a tango band playing in the park while kids were coloring with chalk on the pavement. Pure Neukölln magic.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a kebab shop that opens at midnight and has a line of people debating whether to dip their döner in curry ketchup.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local when they casually avoid eye contact with the guy in the dog costume outside the BART, like it's just another Tuesday.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus today that was half a church choir, half a comedy show. Who knew the 38 had such talent on board?

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists flock to Park Güell, but locals know that the true magic lies in the lesser-known green spaces like Jardins de la Vil.la Cecilia, where silence reigns.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them easily—they're the ones queuing for hours at La Padrina while the locals grab takeout from the tiny places nearby.
